pi_items.source: French medical terminology validation: 'Protocoles' (Protocols) + 'Urgence' (Emergency) = Emergency Protocols directory - standard naming convention for urgent/emergency protocol templates in French medical software
French medical terminology: 'Dépistage' (Screening) + 'COVID19' (COVID-19) + '.html' file extension. Standard naming for COVID-19 screening templates in French medical systems.

French medical standard terminology: Validated against medical literature (HAS - Haute Autorité de Santé guidelines, WHO clinical management documents). Correct French for 'Respiratory Screening Protocol'.

French medical terminology + COVID-19 screening parameter. Verified through HAS guidelines and French health system documentation. Standard vital sign assessment in respiratory screening (fever 77.1% sensitivity per clinical studies).

French medical terminology for SpO2 (peripheral oxygen saturation). Verified through HAS-Santé guidelines and medical literature. Critical parameter in COVID-19 respiratory screening - normal range 95-100%, concerning if <95%.
French medical terminology for 'Cough'. Verified through clinical literature. Cough is a major COVID-19 symptom with 64.7% sensitivity in respiratory screening.

French medical terminology for 'Dyspnea' (shortness of breath). Verified through clinical literature and HAS guidelines. Dyspnea is a primary COVID-19 respiratory symptom with 74.7% sensitivity in screening.

French medical terminology for 'Anosmia' (loss of smell). Verified through peer-reviewed studies (PMC7442001, PMC8709687). Highly specific COVID-19 indicator - low sensitivity but very high specificity with RV+ 3.3.

pi_items.verified_value: Task description mandates 'Anosmie/Agueusie (Oui/Non):' but metadata only requires 'Anosmie'
pi_items.source: Comparison of task.json description field with metadata required_strings. Agueusie (loss of taste) is a verified COVID-19 symptom (frequency ~20%, RV+ 5.8) but NOT included in verifier requirements.

pi_items.verified_value: Task description mandates 'Score de Gravité:' field but it is NOT in required_strings
pi_items.source: Comparison of task.json description with metadata. 'Score de Gravité' (Severity Score) is a real French medical assessment tool documented in medical literature (COVID-SCORE, CoSAS) but NOT checked by verifier.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task involving creation of a COVID-19 respiratory screening template in MedinTux (a real French medical software, version 2.16.012). The metadata specifies a directory named 'Protocoles_Urgence' (Emergency Protocols) and a file 'Depistage_COVID19.html' containing six French medical terms verified as accurate: Temperature, Oxygen Saturation, Cough, Dyspnea, and Anosmia are all standard COVID-19 screening parameters with documented clinical sensitivity values (fever 77.1%, dyspnea 74.7%, cough 64.7%, anosmia high specificity). The task description mentions 'Agueusie' (loss of taste, ~20% prevalence) and 'Score de Gravité' (severity assessment) fields, but these are NOT included in the verifier's required_strings, creating a gap between stated requirements and actual validation criteria.
